meta.clinicianNotes: Simple cerumen impaction — good first-week case for basic otologic history and differentiating conductive symptoms (own voice sounding loud, sudden onset after water exposure) from sensorineural.

patient.name: Keira Symonds
patient.additionalNotes: Keira is easy-going and cooperative, happy to answer questions in a bit of detail without needing much prompting.

history.reasonForAppointment: Right ear suddenly felt completely blocked after a swim at the beach last weekend and hasn't cleared since.

hearing.declineDetails: Right ear went blocked suddenly after swimming about a week ago. Feels muffled, like being underwater. Left ear completely normal. Has used cotton buds regularly for years to 'clean' her ears, more on the right.

earHealth.details: Feeling of fullness and blockage in the right ear since the swim. No pain or discharge. Own voice sounds louder/echoey in that ear.

noiseHistory.details: Office-based work, no significant noise exposure.

history.otherConcerns: Wants to know if it will just clear on its own or if she needs the ear syringed/cleaned, and whether she should stop using cotton buds.
